HDR Calibration

Display Mode

50%
HDR Capabilities Status
Browser API: Checking...
OS & Display: Checking...
Active Mode: SDR Mode
Requires active HDR settings in the OS, and compatible hardware. In Chrome/Edge, enable chrome://flags/#enable-experimental-web-platform-features if needed.
Works Everywhere (SDR & HDR Displays)
  • ✓ Load JXR, OpenEXR, Radiance HDR
  • ✓ Exposure, Calibration & Game Presets
  • ✓ Color Grading & False Color View
  • ✓ SDR & High-Precision HDR Exports
Requires HDR Monitor Mode
  • ⚡ Native Rec.2100 PQ Screen Colors
  • ⚡ Brightness peaks above 100 nits
Setup Instructions
  1. Enable HDR in your system settings (Windows: HDR toggle / macOS: High Dynamic Range checkbox).
  2. In Chrome/Edge, search for chrome://flags/#enable-experimental-web-platform-features and set to Enabled.
  3. Restart the browser and check HDR Monitor Mode above.

Visualizations

Tone Mapping

About this Operator

ACES Filmic

Academy Color Encoding System standard. A high-contrast cinematic S-curve with natural highlight roll-off and rich color retention.

Characteristics: High contrast, cinematic S-curve
Best for / Used in: Cyberpunk 2077, Spider-Man

Export / Conversion

90%

Navigation & Shortcuts

  • Zoom: Mouse wheel / Touchpad
  • Pan: Click and drag image
  • Comparison (Split-Screen): Drag the blue slider dividing left/right on the image
Comparison Slider: Left side is Standard SDR (Clamped), right side is Processed HDR
SDR (Clamped)
HDR (ACES Filmic)
Position X: 0, Y: 0
Luminance (Nits) 0.00 nits
Raw Float RGB R: 0.000, G: 0.000, B: 0.000

Batch Settings

Batch conversion runs mathematically. No HDR monitor or HDR Monitor Mode required.
